Teikoku is from] the Tenchi Muyo OVA, anime and manga franchise

The Grand Galactic Empire of Jurai, also known as the Great Juraian Empire or Takamagahara, is a vast and complex political entity characterized by its unique blend of governance styles. It operates as an absolute imperial, feudal, dynastic, constitutional, federal, semi-democratic, and parliamentary state, making it highly multifaceted in its administration. The empire is notable for being a technologically advanced hyperpower, economic powerhouse, and a dominant force in both trade and military aspects across a large portion of the Milky Way Galaxy. Leadership within the empire rests with the Masaki dynasty, also known as the Juraiouke or the Imperial House of Jurai, who serve as the ruling imperial family. In Japan, where the narrative of the Tenchi Muyo OVA, anime, and manga franchise originates, Jurai is revered almost mythologically, akin to the celestial abode of heavenly gods, or Takamagahara, from Shintoism, Japan's unofficial state religion. This representation of Jurai in Japanese culture positions it as both a legendary entity and a significant focal point within the franchise.

Paraphrase and summarize the following description: the Covenant, a religious theocratic absolute technocratic feudalistic multiracial imperialistic monarchy [the Covenant] which [the Covenant] rules [the Covenant] over [the territories ruled over by the Covenant] the Orion Arm of the Milky Way, from [which franchise the Covenant is from] the Halo video game, anime and book franchise

The Covenant, as described within the Halo video game, anime, and book franchise, is a religious, theocratic, technocratic, feudalistic, and multiracial empire with monarchical governance. This powerful entity exerts control over the Orion Arm of the Milky Way. Its structure and society are defined by a strict hierarchy and are driven by both technological superiority and religious fervor, showcasing the Covenant's unique blend of attributes that govern its imperialistic ambitions in the universe.

The First Galactic Empire, also known as the Galactic Empire or simply "the Empire," is described as a fascist dictatorship or stratocracy in theory, but in practice, it functions as a constitutional imperial, unitary, absolute monarchy with a strong human supremacist and xenophobic stance against aliens. This Empire, featured in the Star Wars series, was not only a dominant technological and military force but also a center for learning, scientific advancement, financial prosperity, and security. It exercised control over the Milky Way galaxy, showcasing its vast reach and power within the narrative of the Star Wars universe.

The Ecumene, from the Halo series, was a highly technologically advanced and post-singularity society with technocratic and aristocratic elements, functioning as a constitutional democratic absolute monarchy. This galactic hyperpower expanded throughout the Milky Way and was governed by the Primoris Prognatus, or Forerunners. The Forerunners were an ancient and advanced race of psychically and magically endowed, highly intelligent humanoids who dominated the Ecumene as its ruling species. Their reign, however, concluded before the advent of the common era, marking the end of their empire in pre-AD times.

Paraphrase and summarize the following description: the galaxy-spanning paramilitary force/meritocratic military organization/pirate guild lead by the infamous race of horned extraterrestrial warmongers known as the Frost Demons [Dragon Ball Z],

In the Dragon Ball Z universe, there exists a formidable galaxy-wide group known as the Frost Demons. This organization functions as a paramilitary force, a meritocratic military entity, and a pirate guild, all rolled into one. It is led by a notorious race of horned alien warmongers, the Frost Demons, renowned for their formidable prowess and aggressive expansion across the galaxy.

Paraphrase and summarize the following description:  the galaxy-spanning paramilitary force/meritocratic military organization/pirate guild lead by the infamous race of horned extraterrestrial warmongers known as the Frost Demons [Dragon Ball Z], the Planetary Trade Organization [Dragon Ball Z],

In the Dragon Ball Z series, the Frost Demons, a notorious race of horned alien warmongers, helm a vast and multifaceted organization known as the Planetary Trade Organization. This entity operates as a combination of a paramilitary force, meritocratic military, and pirate guild, extending its influence across the galaxy. It plays a significant role within the series, showcasing the widespread dominance and fearsome reputation of the Frost Demons.

The Royal House of Einzbern, known in German as Das Königshaus von Einzbern, is a prominent, influential, and somewhat notorious noble, aristocratic, and unofficially royal family from Germany, featured in the Fate/Stay Night franchise. This family wields a significant level of influence across Europe and is distinguished as one of the four great houses within the Clocktower. The Clocktower, situated in London, England, serves as a secretive organization, research facility, and association comprising wealthy, influential noble, and royal families from Europe and parts of Asia, dedicated to the study and propagation of magic and mystical practices.

The distinction of being one of the four great houses of the Clocktower essentially positions the Einzbern family as the de facto royal family of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland. This country, known officially as the Royaume-Uni de Grande-Bretagne et d'Irlande du Nord, operates as a unitary parliamentary constitutional democracy and monarchy. It encompasses the British Isles—comprising England, Wales, Scotland, and Ireland—all ruled by the German-Russian Royal House of Saxe-Gotha-Romanov as its royal family.

Paraphrase and summarize the following description: the feline anthropomorphic race of extraterrestial, hyper-intelligent, psychically and magically gifted with an ability to transform into monsterous tentaclelike god-like beings known as Nyarlotephians [Haiyore! Nyaruko-san]

In the Haiyore! Nyaruko-san series, the Nyarlotephians are depicted as a race of extraterrestrial beings with cat-like anthropomorphic features. They are highly intelligent and possess psychic and magical abilities. Notably, they have the unique power to transform into monstrous, tentacled, god-like entities. This combination of characteristics makes them a distinct and formidable presence within the series.

Paraphrase and summarize the following description: The mixture of steampunk and early diesel technology and sorcery known as Machinart [Mashin-Dōru wa Kizutsukanai], circuits [Machinart] made from spells that [the Machinart circuits] are [the Machinart circuits] put [the Machinart circuits] into objects [the objects which the Machinart circuits are |the Machinart circuits| placed |the Machinart circuits| into] to [the placement of Machinart circuits into objects] bring them [the objects which the Machinart circuits are |the Machinart circuits| placed |the Machinart circuits| into] to life and simultaneously [the placement of Machinart circuits into objects] give them [the objects which the Machinart circuits are |the Machinart circuits| placed |the Machinart circuits| into] a rudimentary form of artificial intelligence, [the development of the Machinart circuits] continues [the development of the Machinart circuits] in the United Kingdom in the early 20th century.

These Automatons [the objects which the Machinart circuits are |the Machinart circuits| placed |the Machinart circuits| into] were [the Automatons] developed as a military weapon by the British government and spread throughout the world; the humans in charge of them [the Automations] became known as puppeteers.

In the series "Mashin-Dōru wa Kizutsukanai," Machinart refers to a unique blend of steampunk, early diesel technology, and sorcery. This technology involves embedding spells in the form of circuits into objects to animate them and endow them with a basic level of artificial intelligence. This process transforms inanimate objects into living entities, known as Automatons, which were initially developed as military weapons by the British government in the early 20th century. These creations spread globally, and those who control them are referred to as puppeteers, marking a significant advancement in both the technological and magical fields within this narrative universe.

The Ashikaga bakufu, also widely recognized as the shogunate, was essentially a form of government in Japan that operated as a de jure absolute imperial royal parliamentary monarchy. However, in reality, it functioned as a feudal royal hereditary military dictatorship. This system was led by the Ashikaga clan, a side branch of the Imperial House of Great Yamato or the Minamoto dynasty, which claims descent from the Shinto pantheon. The government was characterized by its military dictatorship under the leadership of the sei-i taishōgun, or shogun, who wielded significant civil, military, diplomatic, and judicial authority from 1185 to 1868. This period is marked by the shogun carrying out the actual duties of administration, with a strong system of loyalty binding the various powers that supported the bakufu together, including the hereditary military nobility, known as the buke or more famously outside Japan as the samurai. Alongside the samurai were the daimyō, powerful feudal lords and magnates who ruled vast hereditary lands from the 10th century up until the early Meiji period in the mid-19th century, though they remained subordinate to the shogun and nominally to the Emperor. The Ashikaga bakufu's influence extended over Japan and Okinawa and was a significant force behind launching the Imjin Wars.

Paraphrase and summarize the following description: absolute Imperial Royal parliamentary monarchy but de jure feudal royal hereditary military dictatorship of the bakufu [tent government |bakufu|], [the bakufu] popularly [the bakufu] known as [the bakufu’s more popular name] the shogunate, [the bakufu] a system of government in Japan dominated [the bakufu] by a feudal military dictatorship exercised in the name of a sei-i taishōgun [“Commander-in-Chief of the Expeditionary Force Against the Barbarians” |sei-i taishōgun|, the military rulers of Japan |the sei-i taishōgun| during most of the period spanning from 1185 to 1868 in Japanese history who |the sei-i taishōgun| carried out |the sei-i taishōgun| the actual duties of administration in Japan during that time period who |the sei-i taishōgun| also |the sei-i taishōgun| enjoyed civil, military, diplomatic and judicial authority within Japan], [the sei-i taishōgun] more popularly known as [the sei-i taishōgun’s more famous name] a shogun or by the shogun himself, with the hierarchy that held the bakufu together being [the hierarchy holding the bakufu] reinforced by close ties of loyalty between the collection of various powers that supported the bakufu and the hereditary military nobility and officer caste of medieval and early-modern Japan from the late 12th century until their abolition in the late 1870s with high prestige and special privileges, the buke [military families |buke|], [the buke] better known [the buke] outside of Japan and East Asia as [the buke’s other, more famous name] the samurai, the powerful Japanese magnates, and feudal lords who, from the 10th century to the early Meiji period in the middle 19th century ruled most of Japan from their vast, hereditary land holdings despite being subordinate to the shogun/sei-i taishōgun and nominally to the Japanese emperor, whom the buke/samurai served as well-paid retainers, the daimyō [large private land |daimyō]

The bakufu, commonly referred to as the shogunate, was a system of government in Japan that, while theoretically operating as an Imperial Royal parliamentary monarchy, functioned primarily as a feudal royal hereditary military dictatorship. This governance system was in effect from 1185 to 1868 and was led by the sei-i taishōgun, or shogun, a military ruler who held comprehensive civil, military, diplomatic, and judicial powers. The shogunate's structure was supported by a tightly knit hierarchy and loyalty among various powers, including the hereditary military nobility and officer caste known as the buke, or more widely outside Japan as samurai. These samurai, along with powerful magnates and feudal lords known as daimyō, ruled most of Japan from their extensive hereditary lands from the 10th century until the early Meiji period in the mid-19th century. Despite their power and landholdings, these daimyō were technically subordinate to the shogun and, nominally, to the Japanese emperor, serving as highly compensated retainers in a system that continued until its abolition in the late 1870s.

Paraphrase and summarize the following description:   the pre-1662, post-1368, Han Chinese Imperial House of Zhu-ruled absolute dynastic Imperial Royal monarchy of the Great Ming State/Great Ming Empire/"Ming dynasty" ruling over the eighteen provinces of the Han dynasty, Yunnan and Cantonia [Guangdong and Guangxi |Cantonia|] and its [the Great Ming State's] main vassal state, the side branch of the Tang dynasty's imperial family, the Mongol-Manchu Imperial House of Yi, the Han Chinese-Korean Royal House of Jeonju Yi-ruled absolute royal monarchy of the Great Joeson State/Great Joeson/Joeson Kingdom ruling [Joeson] over the entire Korean peninsula, [the Imjin Wars in this timeline] ended with a Japanese victory over both the Great Ming State and Joeson

Before 1662 and after 1368, the Great Ming Empire, also known as the Ming dynasty, was an absolute dynastic imperial monarchy ruled by the Han Chinese Imperial House of Zhu. It governed the eighteen provinces of the former Han dynasty, including Yunnan and the regions of Guangdong and Guangxi, referred to collectively as Cantonia. Additionally, the Ming dynasty maintained sovereignty over a significant vassal state - a branch of the Tang dynasty's imperial family, the Mongol-Manchu Imperial House of Yi. This vassal state was the Korean Kingdom of Great Joseon (also spelled as Joeson), an absolute monarchy under the rule of the Han Chinese-Korean Royal House of Jeonju Yi, which spanned the entire Korean peninsula. In this historical context, the Imjin Wars concluded with a victory for Japan, marking a significant defeat for both the Great Ming State and its vassal, the Kingdom of Joeson.

Paraphrase and summarize the following description: the chief advisor to the reigning members of the Imperial House of Great Yamato/Minamoto dynasty as Japanese Emperors during the reign of the Bakufu over Japan but de facto combined first secretary and regent who assisted an adult Japanese Emperor, the Chief Advisor to the Emperor

The Chief Advisor to the Emperor served as the primary counselor to the emperors from the Imperial House of Great Yamato/Minamoto dynasty during the period when the Bakufu, or shogunate, exerted control over Japan. Functionally, this advisor held a role akin to that of a combined first secretary and regent, providing assistance to an adult Japanese Emperor in matters of governance and policy. This position was crucial in facilitating the smooth operation and administration of the emperor's duties and responsibilities during the shogunate's rule.

Paraphrase and summarize the following description: the officer of the Japanese government under the various bakufu who presided over the Japanese Great Council of State, and controlled the officers of the Japanese state, as well as four great councillors and three minor councillors, who then in turn, controlled other elements of the government, the Chancellor of the Realm,

The Chancellor of the Realm was a significant official in the Japanese government during the times of the various bakufu (shogunate regimes). This official held a pivotal role, presiding over the Japanese Great Council of State. They had authority over state officers, including four major councillors and three junior councillors, who collectively managed the broader elements of the government. This structure ensured centralized control and coordination within the government’s operations under the shogunate's rule.

Paraphrase and summarize the following description: the superconductive, extremely unstable and extremely explosive, radioactive, supermineral of Sakuradite [Code Geass franchise]-powered humanoid war machine mechs standing at an impressive height of four to five meters, equipped with Landspinners, ingenious self-propelled roller skates [Landspinners] affixed to their ankles, [the land spinners] enabling agile mobility and rapid speeds across diverse terrains, capturing data via the remarkable Factsphere Sensors encased within a protective layer of armor,[the Factsphere Sensors] can [the Factsphere Sensors] be [the Factsphere Sensors] retracted to heighten system sensitivity, ensuring the utmost precision and accuracy,[the Factsphere Sensors] endowed with thermographic capabilities and an array of other data-collection functions, all seamlessly collated in real-time, along with having the respective units’ respective cockpits in the respective units’ prominent ‘hump back’, a self-contained control center [the cockpit] from which the mech is expertly piloted, [the cockpit] having the ability to be ejected in case of an emergency known as [the name of the mechs] Knightmare Frames, [Code Geass]

In the Code Geass franchise, Knightmare Frames are advanced humanoid war machines powered by the superconductive, highly unstable, explosive, and radioactive supermineral known as Sakuradite. These mechs stand between four to five meters tall and are equipped with Landspinners - innovative self-propelled roller skates attached to their ankles, enabling them to move swiftly and agilely across various terrains. They are also fitted with advanced Factsphere Sensors, housed within protective armor for data capture. These sensors, which can be retracted to enhance sensitivity, offer exceptional precision and accuracy, including thermographic capabilities and multiple data-collection features, all processed in real-time. The mechs feature cockpits located within their distinctive 'humpback,' serving as a self-contained control center where pilots expertly operate the machines. For safety, this cockpit can be ejected in emergencies, showcasing the sophisticated design and functionality of the Knightmare Frames in the series.

Paraphrase and summarize the following description: The absolute parliamentary constitutional royal imperial monarchy of the German Royal House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha-ruled Holy Brittanian Empire [Code Geass franchise] ruling [the Holy Brittanian Empire] over the Southern United States, all of the Lesser Antilies, French Guyana, Suriname, Egypt, Sudan, Malyasia, Brunei, Singapore, Greater India [Afghanistan and India] and Oceania [Australia and New Zealand] from [the Holy Brittanian Empire’s homebase] Canada and Greater Jamaica [Jamaica, the Turks and Caicos, Belize and the Cayman Islands

In the Code Geass franchise, the Holy Britannian Empire operates as an absolute parliamentary constitutional monarchy under the rule of the German Royal House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha. This empire extends its dominion over a vast area, including the Southern United States, all of the Lesser Antilles, French Guiana, Suriname, Egypt, Sudan, Malaysia, Brunei, Singapore, Greater India (encompassing Afghanistan and India), and Oceania (comprising Australia and New Zealand). Its administration is conducted from its bases in Canada and Greater Jamaica, which includes Jamaica itself along with the Turks and Caicos, Belize, and the Cayman Islands, illustrating its wide-reaching influence and control.

In the Muv-Luv franchise, Tactical Surface Fighters (TSFs) are described as oversized fighter planes that are coated in an anti-projectile and heat-resistant composite material. This material is further enhanced with an anti-laser coating, enabling the TSFs to operate under various conditions. These advanced machines are equipped with high-tech weaponry, including particle beam cannons and plasma blasters, and are capable of operating in space as well as flying within Earth's atmosphere. TSFs are nearly indestructible by conventional weapons and have the unique ability to transform into oversized humanoid mechs. In their mech form, they offer all-purpose maneuverability, combining bipedal motion and rocket thrust for movement. They utilize stealth and superior mobility to outmaneuver any opponent on the battlefield, showcasing their versatility and advanced technological design.

In the Magika Kenshi to Shōkan Maō series, Divas are essentially contracted spirits that embody creatures, concepts, or characters from various mythologies or folk beliefs. These spirits form contracts with specific human or supernatural beings, typically females aged between 13 to 29. The bond between a Diva and its partner is symbolized by a mark known as a Stigma, usually found on the contractor's right hand. 

Through this contract, the contractor gains the ability to materialize and wield a Décolleté Oblique/Decorteo Brigieux, a type of magical armor that often resembles the revealing attire of knights or mages commonly featured in visual novels, anime, manga, and JRPGs. This armor can only be utilized by female humans or supernatural entities who have entered into a contract with a Diva and appears when the contractor is engaged in battle or using a significant amount of the Diva's power. Additionally, the contract grants the contractor the ability to harness the Diva's magical capabilities, enhancing their combat effectiveness and abilities in battle.

In the IS: Infinite Stratos series, IS units refer to highly sophisticated powered exoskeletons initially designed for space exploration. These units boast technology and combat capabilities unmatched by any other existing weapon system. They come equipped with nearly indestructible shield barriers, 100% optical camouflage that renders them invisible, and the ability to travel at hypersonic speeds. Additionally, they can perform highly complex, high G maneuvers without being affected by gravity-induced blackouts or red-outs, and they are capable of space flight. A unique aspect of IS units is that their operation is limited exclusively to pre-teen girls, teenage girls, and women up to early middle-age, making them a distinct element within the IS: Infinite Stratos franchise.
